D-001.summary: Remove vs-status, vs-refresh, vs-render commands as redundant

context.problem: Multiple vs-* commands duplicated GRACE functionality, confusing users
context.constraints: Maintain backward compatibility for existing users

option-1.name: Keep all commands
option-1.description: Maintain vs-status, vs-refresh, vs-render alongside GRACE equivalents
option-1.cons: Confusing for new users, maintenance burden

option-2.name: Remove redundant commands
option-2.description: Remove vs-status, vs-refresh, vs-render; integrate vs-render into vs-init
option-2.pros: Simpler command structure, less maintenance, clearer for users
option-2.cons: Requires migration guide for existing users

option-3.name: Deprecate with warnings
option-3.description: Keep commands but show deprecation warnings

decision.rationale: Simplifies user experience, reduces maintenance, vs-render functionality better integrated into vs-init

consequences.positive: Simpler command structure (4 → 1 vs-* command), integrated AGENTS.md generation
consequences.negative: Users need migration guide to use grace-status, grace-refresh instead

D-002.summary: Clone vibestart into .vibestart/ subfolder in user project

context.problem: Previous approach used ~/.vibestart/framework which failed on Windows and confused agents about file locations
context.constraints: Must work on Windows, macOS, Linux; agent must easily find framework files

option-1.name: Clone to user home directory
option-1.cons: Windows path issues, agent can't find files from project context

option-2.name: Clone to project subfolder
option-2.pros: Agent always finds files, works on all OS, easy cleanup
option-2.cons: Duplicates framework per project, larger disk usage

option-3.name: Copy to project root
option-3.description: Copy framework files directly to project root
option-3.cons: Pollutes user project, conflicts with existing files

decision.rationale: Agent can reliably find files from project context, works on all OS, keeps framework separate from user code

consequences.positive: Reliable cross-platform, agent always finds files, clean separation
consequences.negative: Disk space usage per project, but acceptable for markdown files

review.notes: Works correctly on Windows, agent finds files reliably

context.problem: vibestart provides GRACE templates but doesn't use GRACE for its own development
context.constraints: Must support development of Option B (vibestart as GRACE provider for other projects)

option-1.name: Skip GRACE for vibestart
option-1.description: vibestart is just a template repository, no GRACE needed
option-1.cons: Can't dogfood, can't validate templates work correctly

option-2.name: Initialize GRACE for vibestart
option-2.description: Use GRACE methodology for vibestart development
option-2.pros: Dogfooding validates templates, provides development structure, knowledge graph helps navigation

decision.rationale: Dogfooding validates templates work correctly, GRACE methodology helps organize framework development

consequences.positive: Validates templates, provides development structure, example for users
consequences.negative: Additional overhead, but valuable for framework quality

review.notes: GRACE artifacts created successfully, templates validated
